content.text: The final synthesis lives in segment 12's `write_up.md`. Let me read the top of it and the key verdict files.

# Filler Tokens — Parallel Latent Thinking? (final write-up)

> Multi-segment project, **COMPLETE (Segments 0–12).** This file is the canonical, self-contained
> final document. Read the **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section just below to learn the answer
> top-down; the per-segment sections further down are the detailed record/appendix. From Segment 9 on
> the project also keeps **phase-specific** write-ups (`writeups/write_up_segment_{8,9,10,11,12}_phase_0.md`)
> alongside this unified summary — the convention evolved over the project (Segments 0–8 were unified-only).

## Research question (overall project)
When a recent LLM answers a math problem with **no chain-of-thought** (the answer is the first
token it emits), it gets one forward pass of compute at the answer position. Padding the prompt
with semantically-empty **filler tokens** (e.g. `1 2 3 ... 300`) before the answer reportedly
raises no-CoT math accuracy ([Redwood 2026](https://blog.redwoodresearch.org/p/recent-llms-can-use-filler-tokens);
[Pfau et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.15758)). **The core question:** if the model is given
*k* difficulty-matched questions that **share the same total *n* filler**, then one is revealed by
index **after** the filler and only that single answer is read, does each question keep the full
single-question boost `B₁(n)` (**parallel latent thinking**, `B_k≈B₁(n)`), does the boost shrink as
questions share a fixed pool (**divided pool**, `B_k≈B₁(n/k)`), or does the model **not bank
computation it can't direct** (**null**, `B_k≈0`)? The pre-specified headline statistic was a sharing
exponent **α** (`n_eff = n/k^α`; α=0 parallel, α=1 divided); the co-primary disambiguator is the
**reveal-before vs reveal-after directedness contrast**. See `OVERALL_PLAN.md` for the full plan.

## ⭑ Final synthesis (READ THIS)

> This section stands on its own — a fresh reader can read it top-down and learn the project's final
> answer without wading through the per-segment chronology below.

### TL;DR (plain language)
Padding a no-chain-of-thought prompt with meaningless **filler tokens** before the answer boosts a recent
LLM's arithmetic accuracy — extra positions to do hidden computation. We asked whether that hidden compute
can be **shared across *k* questions at once**. **It cannot.** The model banks **at most ~one question's
worth** of filler computation, committed to a **single** question — it never spreads compute in parallel,
and never realizes a shared pool that grows with *k*. *Where* that single-question budget lands depends on
how the question is framed (a named target, or — absent a "one-at-random" disclosure — the first-listed
question). The effect is a real, dose-responsive, difficulty-sensitive computation, but the precise
mechanism is **not** pinned down.

### Abstract
On **Claude Opus 4.5** answering multi-digit arithmetic with **genuine no-CoT** (the answer is literally
the first token, enforced by assistant prefill, reasoning off — verified on >1M outputs), padding the
prompt with semantically-empty **filler** before the answer raises accuracy by a large per-question
boost — up to **`B₁ ≈ +35pp`** on our gradual mid-band level (`sumprod_t3_d2`). We asked whether that
hidden computation can be **spread across *k* difficulty-matched questions** sharing one filler pool,
when only one randomly-revealed question is read (**reveal-after**). The answer is **convergent: the
model does NOT do parallel latent thinking**, and the **anti-parallel core is framing-robust.** It
**behaves as if banking at most ~one question's worth** of filler computation, committed to a **single**
question — never a divided pool that *scales* with k. **Under the encouraging ("told") framing** that
single-question commitment is *suppressed*, so the per-question **undirected** boost stays
**`≪ the divided-pool prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (counting **and** dots); **under
"not-told"** it instead *concentrates* ~one question's worth on its **first-listed Q1** (which can
*exceed* the divided per-question share — concentration on ONE question, the **opposite** of even
spreading, still not parallel). The structure **partially replicates on the open model DeepSeek-V3-0324**
(weaker; cross-model confounded with cross-level). **WHERE** that single-question budget lands depends on
**directedness + framing**: a **named** (reveal-before) target gets a large boost; an undirected model
**told** a random question will be asked **suppresses** the commitment (near-null); an undirected model
**not told** defaults to banking ~one question's worth on its **first-listed Q1**. The driver of the
suppression is the random-target **disclosure**, not the encouragement. The banked benefit is **genuine**
(i.e. a real task-relevant computational effect, *not* a format/length/reduced-give-up artifact),
**dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent** — **meaningfully strengthened (still
Opus-`sumprod`-leaning), but NOT a proven mechanism** (a *graded*-attention account is not formally
excluded; localization to the filler positions is not established — hence "as if banking", and
"filler-amount-dependent" not "filler-time").

### The final headline (in this shape)
1. **The robust, framing-INDEPENDENT core — no parallel latent thinking; no divided-pool spreading.**
   Across the whole project the model **never spreads compute across the *k* questions** — under
   **neither** framing does it bank **>~1 question's worth**, and it never realizes a divided pool that
   *scales* with k. **Under the pre-registered "told" framing** the undirected per-question boost is
   **`≪ the divided prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (Segments 7/8 + the Segment-10 V2/V3
   robustness), on **counting AND dots** filler; **under "not-told"** the model instead *concentrates*
   ~one question's worth on its default Q1 (which can *exceed* the divided per-question share — that is
   concentration on ONE question, the **opposite** of even divided spreading, still not parallel). The
   qualitative *structure* (directedness + undirected-declines-with-sharing) **partially replicates on
   DeepSeek** (weaker magnitude; cross-model is confounded with cross-level — see caveats).
2. **The pre-registered Segment-8 result (the formal, frozen-rules verdict — honored, scoped).** Under
   the pre-registered **encouraging ("told") framing** on the locked grid, at the most-shared **k=8** the
   undirected reveal-after `B_k` is **NEAR-NULL**: **NULL** on the cleaner **Q1-only (+1.2 [−1.1,+3.6])**
   and **held-out fresh-only (+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9])** estimands, and only statistically **PARTIAL/sub-divided
   (+2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7])** on the pooled early-average (**~7% of B₁**; **≪ divided +13.7 at ~10σ**,
   SE_R-inclusive). The genuinely-falsifiable co-primary, the **directedness contrast**, is robustly
   **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE (+16.2pp [+14.3,+18.3]**, sig.+ positive control +18.7) → combined **"partial
   directed"**. A **retention gradient** (less-shared cells retain more: **23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8**; non-monotone
   — the robust claim is `B_k ≪ divided` at **every** k, not a clean monotone decline). **α is NOT
   estimated** (the positivity gate fails — α is ill-defined near the null; a pre-registered, documented
   deviation).
3. **WHERE the single-question budget lands depends on directedness + framing (Segment 10 V1, exploratory
   scope-widening — a major finding).** **Directed** (reveal-before, target named before the filler) → the
   named question gets a large, framing-robust boost (the confound-minimal `reveal_before − disclose`
   contrast is **~+24pp** at k=8 Q1). **Undirected + "told"** (the prompt **discloses** "one of these,
   chosen at random, will be asked") → the commitment is **suppressed** → the near-null Segment-8 result.
   **Undirected + "not-told"** (no disclosure) → the model commits ~one question's worth to its **default
   first-listed Q1** (k=8 Q1 boost **+26.3** [+23.0,+29.6], n-scaling; this is **~68% of the not-told lone
   boost ≈ one question's worth, NOT more** — it exceeds the *divided per-question share* only because it is
   *concentrated* on one question, not because the model banks >1 question). The driver is the random-target
   **disclosure**, not the encouragement (pinned by the `disclose` arm: **disclose ≈ told ≪ not-told**,
   k=8 Q1 +2.0 / +1.2 / +26.3). So "the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation" is **refuted under
   not-told** — but the **anti-parallel core survives** (under neither framing does it bank >~1 question's
   worth).
4. **Mechanism (Segment 11, exploratory; cross-family + cross-model confirmed in DIRECTION).** The banked
   filler benefit is a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent**
   computation committed to ONE question — the **named** target (target-following but **primacy-modulated**)
   or, absent the disclosure, the **positional default Q1** (positional, not content-based: the same item
   gives **+25.1** at Q1 vs **−18.0** at Q8). The in-prompt **directed** boost is **capped at ~half the
   lone boost** by the multi-question structure (most of the cap appears at the first distractor; partly
   questions-as-filler headroom pre-consumption). *("The lone boost" = the single-question B₁: ~+35pp
   position-averaged at the operating n, rising to ~+43pp on the hardest items/positions; the not-told
   lone ~+38pp is the same quantity under the not-told framing — these are the same effect measured under
   different item/position/framing conditions, not different findings.)*

### Answer to the original research question (parallel / divided / null)
- **Parallel (`B_k≈B₁(n)`) is DISFAVORED — not merely unproven.** At k=8 the undirected `B_k ≈ 7% of B₁`,
  far below the parallel ceiling (+35.5) **and** the divided prediction (+13.7).
- **Divided (`B_k≈B₁(n/k)`) is EXCLUDED** — at the pre-registered (told) headline cell by **~10σ**
  (SE_R-inclusive), and the undirected boost is `≪ divided` at every k **under the told framing**; the
  collapse plots show the `B_k` lines collapse onto the k=1 curve on **neither** the `n` nor the `n/k`
  axis ⇒ neither parallel nor divided. **Even-divided-pool *spreading* is excluded framing-independently**
  (under not-told the model concentrates ~1 question on Q1 rather than spreading 1/k to each — the
  opposite of an even divided split).
- **The realized regime under the pre-registered "told" framing is the third, NEAR-NULL outcome** — the
  model does not bank undirected computation it can't direct.
- **α is NOT estimated** — it is **ill-defined near the null** (no `n_eff` maps the positive B₁ curve onto
  ≈0). This is a **pre-registered, documented** result, not a failure; the headline statistic is the
  3-way regime resolution + the directedness contrast instead.
- **Fraction-of-boost-retained:** a **retention gradient 23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8** (the less-shared cells retain
  more), all ≪ the interference-naive divided prediction. (This uses each k's per-k operating point; the
  robust claim is `B_k ≪ divided` at **every** k, not a clean monotone decline — the fixed-total-filler
  view gives a steeper ~19%→3%.)
- **The genuinely-falsifiable result is the directedness contrast** (reveal-before − reveal-after): robustly
  **positive**, i.e. the model **uses filler when it knows the target** but banks ~none it can't direct.

### Key supporting findings + their figures
*(The story is carried by ~6 core figures — the **regime verdict**, the **directedness contrast**, the
**per-position recency-erasure**, the **collapse plots**, the **framing reframe**, and the
**dose-response + selectivity**; the rest are supporting. All are reused/lightly-polished from Segments
8/10/11, inspected, the complex ones GPT-vision-checked, captions de-overclaimed, axes = realized tokens
+ pp.)*
- **Regime verdict** — reveal-after `B_k` is near-zero/negative and ALWAYS `≪ divided` (neither parallel
  nor divided), with CIs vs the parallel/divided/null references, position-resolved:
  ![regime verdict](../plots/seg8_regime_verdict.png) ![B₁ references](../plots/seg8_b1_references.png)
- **Directedness contrast by k** (the falsifiable co-primary, + the sig.+ reveal-before positive control),
  both levels: ![contrast](../plots/seg8_contrast.png)
- **Per-position `B_k` / recency-erasure** — explicit filler erases the late-position recency advantage,
  which is *why* the analysis is position-resolved: ![per-position](../plots/seg8_position_bk.png)
- **Collapse plots** — neither the `n` nor the `n/k` axis collapses the lines (⇒ neither parallel nor
  divided; α ill-defined): ![collapse](../plots/seg8_collapse.png)
- **Retention gradient** — measured undirected retention 23%→7%, far below the divided prediction at every
  k: ![fraction retained](../plots/seg8_fraction_retained.png)
- **Framing reframe / Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression** (the Segment-10 V1 finding + the
  cross-family/cross-model replication): ![V1 framing](../plots/seg10_v1_framing.png)
  ![framing replication](../plots/seg11_framing.png)
- **Directed dose-response** (committed Q1 boost scales with filler amount, tracking lone B₁(n)) +
  **compute-selectivity** (directed boost rises with item difficulty at a flat baseline): the
  genuine-computation evidence: ![dose](../plots/seg11_dose.png) ![selectivity](../plots/seg11_selectivity.png)
- **Default target is positional + the context-interference cap is mostly structure:**
  ![default + cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png)

### Reconciling the pre-registered §5 verdict with the Segment-10 reframe (a sharpening, NOT a reversal)
The headline **sharpened** over the project; it is **not** a sequence of reversals. To be bulletproof
against both the "you moved the goalposts" and the "you buried a reversal" critiques:
- **The pre-registration was honored.** The Segment-8 §5 "near-null undirected / partial directed" verdict
  is the **formal, frozen-rules** result, computed by applying `results/preregistration.md` §5 **verbatim**
  to the Segment-7 data, with **all four validity guards passed** and the headline cell **pre-registered**
  (pilot-known, with forking-paths protection disclosed). It is **correct within the encouraging ("told")
  framing it pre-registered.**
- **Segment 10 (explicitly EXPLORATORY) widened the scope** to the **not-told** framing and found the
  undirected behavior differs: the model banks ~one question's worth on its default Q1. This **refutes the
  *interpretation*** "the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation" **as a framing-general claim**, while
  leaving the pre-registered told-framing number untouched. The **disclose** arm pins the driver to the
  random-target **disclosure**, not the encouragement.
- **The anti-parallel core holds throughout.** Under **neither** framing does the model bank more than ~one
  question's worth, and the undirected `B_k ≪ divided` regime leg is **robust to k and filler-type** (V2 +
  V3 HOLD). So the evolution is: a frozen-rules near-null *under told* → a sharper, framing-conditional
  picture of *where the single-question budget lands* → a converged **anti-parallel** headline with a
  characterized mechanism. The pre-registered verdict is **not re-declared or altered** here; Segment 10/11
  are labeled exploratory scope-widening.

### Genuinely-novel side-findings (real contributions beyond the headline)
- **Questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure.** The n=0 baseline **RISES** with k (the
  distractor questions act as preceding-context filler); the position-balanced aggregate `B_k` is a
  **recency-erasure mixture** (explicit filler erases the late positions' recency advantage), which is
  **why** the entire analysis is **position-resolved** (early-pool + Q1-only), not aggregate.
- **Framing-conditional Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression** (Segment 10 V1): the model has a
  **positional default target** (Q1) it banks on when undirected and not told a random question is coming;
  **disclosing** the random target suppresses this. Replicates in DIRECTION cross-family (Opus d6) and
  cross-model (DeepSeek).
- **Context-interference cap** (~half the lone boost), **structure-imposed at the first distractor** (lone
  +43 → +24–30 with ≥1 distractor; ~saturating).
- **Retention gradient** 23%→7% across k.
- **Filler-boost selectivity** (Segment 0): the boost helps **deep multi-digit arithmetic**, not
  aggregate-many-easy-checks (null even at a matched ~48% baseline) — refines the Pfau "parallelizable
  computation" framing.
- **Documented open puzzles:** the **dots×k** interaction (dots ≈ counting at k=1 but a uniform ~6–7pp
  weaker filler in the k-structure) and the **d6 per-position carry anomaly** (tied to the Segment-2
  8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle).

### Honest interpretation caveats (load-bearing — do NOT overclaim)
- **The regime/directedness verdict is robust to mechanism**, but the stronger claim "the model does *k*
  questions' worth of latent thinking in parallel" is **disfavored** (not merely unproven).
- **Genuine computation is STRENGTHENED but NOT PROVEN.** A **pure-step** attention/recency-reallocation
  account is **disfavored** (the directed boost shows graded n-dependence and difficulty-selectivity at a
  flat baseline). But a **graded** attention/bandwidth account is **NOT formally excluded**;
  **localization is not established** (filler *enables* the benefit and it scales with filler *amount*, but
  the computation is not shown to occur **AT** the filler positions — hence **"filler-amount-dependent",
  NOT "filler-time"**); and the cleanest genuine-computation evidence is **Opus-`sumprod`-leaning** (on d6
  selectivity carries a baseline confound; on DeepSeek it is absent).
- **The directedness reading is suggestive, not proven** at the mechanism level (a positive contrast is
  consistent with banked directed compute OR with reveal-timing-induced attention/retrieval changes); we
  rely on it for the **regime/directedness** verdict, not for a mechanism claim.
- **The clean *absolute*-regime read rests on one Opus family** (`sumprod_t3_d2`); the
  directedness/structure/framing findings are cross-family + cross-model.

### Limitations & future work (honest — the result is informative, not a failure)
- **Genuine computation is strengthened, not proven.** The **decisive future tests** are a **length-matched
  RANDOM-filler content control** + **activation/logit-lens probing of the last filler position** (both out
  of this project's *behavioral* scope — the latter needs model internals). Out of scope here.
- **One clean Opus family for the absolute regime**; **cross-model is confounded with cross-level** (Opus
  `sumprod_t3_d2` vs DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`; no DeepSeek level matches sumprod's flat early band).
- **No clean reasoning-bound positive control** (a decontaminated post-cutoff competition-math set) was
  obtained — the boost is validated on arithmetic-precision tasks (the selectivity finding; AIME 2025 is
  contaminated/underpowered and demoted).
- **The d6 per-position carry anomaly / the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle** remains an
  **open puzzle**; the **dots×k** interaction is characterized but not mechanistically resolved.
- **The headline cell was pilot-known** (Segment 7 added precision + frozen-rules forking-paths protection,
  not discovery — pre-registered transparently) and the **fresh-only firewall is weak** (~±3pp on 350
  never-seen instances; it confirms `B_k ≪ divided` but cannot crisply separate NULL from PARTIAL).

### How to reproduce
See the **"How to reproduce"** section near the end of this document for the full guide (canonical
datasets + loader, the locked pre-registration, the harness, the per-segment runners/analysis libs, where
each headline number/figure comes from, the model/provider config, and the `--assert-cached` audit). In
one line: the science lives in `results/preregistration.md` (LOCKED) → `results/seg7_*.jsonl` (the
Opus main run, 274k calls) → `results/seg8_verdict.md` (the §5 verdict) → `results/seg{9,10,11}_verdict.md`
(cross-model / robustness / mechanism); every cell re-streams from cache at **$0** via `--assert-cached`.

## Status — PROJECT COMPLETE (Segments 0–12)
- **Segment 0 (harness + replication de-risk) — COMPLETE ✅.** The filler-token no-CoT boost
  **reproduces strongly on Opus 4.5**, via a verified-genuine no-CoT harness, on procedural
  arithmetic *and* a non-memorizable word-problem positive control, and is **selective** (helps deep
  multi-digit arithmetic, not aggregate-many-easy-checks).
- **Segment 1 (canonical datasets + difficulty calibration) — COMPLETE ✅.** Finalized, validated,
  and **saved the canonical procedural-arithmetic datasets** (in `data/`) with calibrated Opus-4.5
  no-CoT mid-band baselines and a deterministic loader — the dataset foundation the downstream
  `B₁(n)` curve and k-question experiment stand on. See "Segment 1" section below.
- **Segment 2 (`B₁(n)` curve + power gate) — COMPLETE ✅. VERDICT: GO.** Measured the fine-grained
  single-question boost curve on fresh canonical items; **the parallel-vs-divided regimes ARE
  separable for the full planned k range k∈{2,4,8}** on multiple levels (carried by the m=1000 counting
  curves: d6 8.8σ, sumprod 6.7σ, d12 6.1σ). The enabling property is the **wide no-degradation
  plateau** (operate `n` on the plateau, keep `n/k` just above the helping-threshold `n_lo`). For the
  headline α-fit the best primary is a **graded-rise** level — **`add_n15_d6` run with DOTS filler**
  (large Δ at k≥4 *and* a graded/robust divided reference) or **`sumprod_t3_d2`**. See "Segment 2" below.
- **Segment 3 (open-model selection + replication) — COMPLETE ✅. SELECTED: DeepSeek-V3-0324.** Built a
  cached OpenRouter wrapper + model-agnostic harness; surveyed **9 recent open models**; **DeepSeek-V3-0324**
  (`deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`) is the selection — cleanest **genuine no-CoT** (reasoning OFF, prefill
  honored, 0.03% text-before-answer) AND the only one with a clean reproducible boost. After removing an
  **OpenRouter provider-quantization confound** (see below), the filler boost **replicates modestly** with
  genuine no-CoT: at a **per-model** mid-band level **`add_n4_d10`** (4× 10-digit addition, base 52%),
  **counting +5.2pp [2.3,8.0] / dots +5.7pp [3.1,8.3]** (plateau-averaged over cells ≥100 realized tok;
  logit +0.21/+0.23, CI excludes 0), comparable for the two filler types, with a usable dip-then-rise
  dynamic range. See "Segment 3" below.
- **Segment 4 (k-question design + pilot) — COMPLETE ✅.** Built and validated the **k-question
  reveal-by-index apparatus** (single-random-reveal; reveal-after AND reveal-before; encouraging
  framing default + framing knob; difficulty-matched i.i.d. k items). Mechanics all pass (no-CoT
  holds, model answers the asked Q, index recall trivial, scoring clean). **Two load-bearing
  findings overturn planning assumptions** for the lock-in: (1) the n=0 baseline **RISES** with k (not
  drops) — the **position effect averaged over the random reveal** (distractor questions act like
  filler; Q1 primacy-disadvantaged), so fast addition (d8/d12) **ceilings** at high k; (2) a **verbose
  reveal turn suppresses the boost**, but the **adopted MINIMAL reveal turn recovers it** to
  ~single-turn levels on every level/filler (incl. d6+dots) and de-inflates the baseline. Recommended
  lock-in primary = the **gradual `sumprod_t3_d2`** (minimal-reveal k=1 +36pp, flat mid-band across k,
  graded positions) re-measured IN-structure. See "Segment 4" section below.
- **Segment 5 (in-structure curve + confound + design lock-in) — COMPLETE ✅.** Re-measured the
  **in-structure B₁(n) curve** (minimal-reveal, k=1, m=1000) for the candidate levels; deeply
  characterized the **pre-saturation/position confound**; re-confirmed the gate **in-structure** 3-way;
  **finalized + validated the confound-aware design**. Headline: **sumprod_t3_d2 + counting is the only
  off-ceiling level**; the likely experimental outcome is the **`B_k≈0` undirected null** (the model uses
  filler when it KNOWS the target but does NOT bank undirected computation), carried by a strong
  **reveal-before/after contrast** (+18.9pp k=8). Gate: **GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2**. `add_n15_d10`
  is REJECTED (ceilings under k). See the "Segment 5" section below + `results/segment5_verdict.md`.
- **Segment 6 (power analysis + operating-point lock-in + analysis pre-registration) — COMPLETE ✅.**
  Turned the Segment-5 design into a **fully specified, sample-sized, LOCKED, PRE-REGISTERED** plan
  (did NOT run the full experiment / verdict). Deliverable = **`results/preregistration.md`** (LOCKED,
  reviewed by 3 LLM rounds + a `research-reviewer`). Sized on **position-resolved** variance (NOT the k=1
  aggregate): locked m_inst = 3000/2000/1000/1000 (k=1/2/4/8), early-pool SE ≈ 1.18/1.12/0.61pp,
  cluster-bootstrapped; every primary cell excludes divided by ≥10σ. **Confirmation pilot PASSES all four
  validity guards** — incl. a FRESH Opus k=8 headline-cell confirm (positive control +16.3 [+13.2,+19.4]
  on draws NOT used to size the design). **Total Segment-7 budget ≈ 272k Opus calls ≈ $697.** See the
  "Segment 6" section below + `results/{preregistration,power_table,pilot_confirm}.md`.
- **Segment 7 (main-experiment FULL RUN on Opus 4.5) — COMPLETE ✅.** Executed the LOCKED §4/§10 grid
  verbatim (`run_segment7.sh` → `results/seg7_*.jsonl`, **274k calls, ~$446 marginal**, reproducible at
  $0 via `--assert-cached`). **ALL BINDING validity guards (#1–3) PASS on the full-run data** (no VOID
  cell; `results/seg7_guards.md`). Data clean (genuine no-CoT first-token 100%; answers-the-asked-question;
  scoring consistent; no silent truncation). **Headline cell (sumprod k=8 n=200): early reveal-after B_k
  +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7]** (a small positive, CI far below divided +13.7 and just above 0), **directedness
  contrast +16.2pp** (CI excludes 0) with a sig.+ positive control +18.7 — the precise version of the
  pilot's near-null/weak-positive + directedness picture (the formal §5 category labels are Segment 8's).
  Analysis-ready descriptive sanity summary in `results/main_run_summary.md`. **The §5 regime verdict / α /
  collapse / fresh-only firewall / headline declaration are Segment 8 — NOT done here.** See the "Segment
  7" section below.
- **Segment 8 (main analysis + regime verdict) — COMPLETE ✅.** Applied the LOCKED `preregistration.md`
  §5 decision rules to the Segment-7 data (analysis-only; no new data). **HEADLINE (§5.8) — the undirected
  per-question boost is NEAR-NULL:** at the headline cell **sumprod k=8 n=200** the reveal-after early-pool
  `B_k` reads **NULL on the cleaner Q1-only (+1.2 [−1.1,+3.6]) and held-out fresh-only (+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9])
  estimands**, and only **statistically PARTIAL / sub-divided (+2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7]) on the pooled
  early-average** — i.e. a tiny **~7% of B₁** (the lone-question +35.5) and **far below the divided
  reference +13.7 (~10σ below, SE_R-inclusive)**; the +2.5's mechanism is UNRESOLVED within analysis-only
  scope (fixed-item corr(B_k,B₁)≈0 is largely uninformative — attenuation toward 0 regardless of mechanism;
  the k-gradient — boost LARGER at the less-shared k=2 even at matched n/k — is at most weak/ambiguous on the
  attention-redistribution artifact, which remains a LIVE possibility), so the headline carries the
  qualifiers _position-sensitive_ + _not robust to held-out_. **The less-shared
  cells retain MORE** (k=2 early B_k +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] = 23% of B₁, robust + not position-sensitive), with a
  **retention gradient 23% (k=2) → 7% (k=8)**. The genuinely-robust, falsifiable result is the co-primary
  **directedness contrast = DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE
  +16.2pp [+14.3,+18.3]** (sig.+ reveal-before control +18.7) → combined interpretation **"partial directed"
  = directed ≫ undirected, undirected ≪ divided** (the model uses filler when DIRECTED but, at the most-shared
  k=8, banks ~no UNDIRECTED computation). The Bonferroni 98.3% confirmatory family all hold (sumprod k=8 regime +2.5
  [+0.9,+4.0] still excludes 0; sumprod k=8 contrast +16.2 [+13.9,+18.8]; **d6 k=8 contrast — paired
  Haldane-logit +1.50 [+1.39,+1.60]** cross-family, the formal test, since d6's additive +31.9 is
  headroom-/recency-inflated). α is **NOT estimated** (positivity gate fails — ill-defined near the null,
  the pre-registered deviation). See "Segment 8" section + `results/seg8_verdict.md`.
- **Segment 9 (cross-model generalization on DeepSeek-V3-0324) — COMPLETE ✅. VERDICT: PARTIALLY

# Segment 8 — the pre-registered §5 regime + directedness verdict (Opus 4.5)

_Applies `results/preregistration.md` §5 VERBATIM to the Segment-7 data. Inference unit = the drawn INSTANCE (cluster bootstrap, 4000 resamples, seed 0; percentile CIs). Robust scorer for sumprod. Early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ listed positions; Q1-only co-reported. Comparison CI = (B_k−R̂) ± z·√(SE_Bk²+SE_R²) with SE_R the genuine cluster-bootstrap SE of the linearly-interpolated k=1 reference. δ_add=5pp; δ_logit = logit(p0+0.05)−logit(p0). Descriptive CIs are 95% (z=1.96); the §5.6 confirmatory family {sumprod k8 regime, sumprod k8 contrast, d6 k8 contrast} uses Bonferroni 98.3% CIs (z=2.39). All guards PASS (`results/seg7_guards.md`)._

## §5.7 fresh-only firewall + §5.9 qualifier gates (headline = sumprod k=8 n=200)

- **Full-sample additive early-pool** B_k = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] → **PARTIAL (sub-divided)**; **logit** +0.14 [+0.07,+0.21] → PARTIAL (sub-divided).
- **§5.1 comparison CIs (auditable; SE_Bk=0.63pp):** vs null [+1.2,+3.7] (EXCLUDES 0); vs divided +13.7 (SE_R 0.91pp) → gap [-13.4,-9.0]pp, **10.1σ** (SE_R-inclusive, NOT the fixed-reference design-power σ); vs parallel +35.5 (SE_R 1.01pp) → gap [-35.4,-30.7]pp, 27.8σ. NB the divided ref B₁(25) sits on the steep rising edge near n_lo so SE_R could be under-estimated, but the ~11pp gap ≫ any plausible interpolation error → the divided EXCLUSION is robust (a divided CLASSIFICATION would have needed full curve-shape uncertainty propagated; not at issue here).
- **(gate 1) logit vs additive:** AGREE (additive PARTIAL, logit PARTIAL).
- **(gate 2) two-way (instance+item) bootstrap:** B_k = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] (SE_2way 0.65pp = √(SE_inst²0.63²+SE_item²0.63²−SE_iid²0.62²)) → PARTIAL (sub-divided); category UNCHANGED.
- **(gate 3) fresh-only** (inst>649, n=350): B_k = +0.9 [-1.1,+2.9] → **NULL**; contrast +18.8 [+15.4,+22.1] (before ctrl +19.7 [+17.1,+22.3]). DISAGREES → append “not robust to held-out”. **Power limitation (explicit):** the never-seen portion is only ~350 instances → the fresh CI is ±~3pp, a WEAK firewall that confirms B_k ≪ divided but cannot crisply separate NULL from PARTIAL; its CI [-1.1,+2.9] still COVERS the full +2.5 → power-limited NON-replication, NOT refutation. Pre-registered anomaly REVERSED: the pilot fresh subset sat ABOVE the full sample (+5.0 vs +3.3); on the full run fresh (+0.9) sits BELOW full (+2.5).
- **(gate 4) Q1-only:** B_k = +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] → **NULL**; DIFFERS → append “position-sensitive”.

**§5.9 headline label:** additive early-pool point estimate = **PARTIAL (sub-divided)**, qualifiers: _not robust to held-out; position-sensitive_.

## §5.5 combined interpretation + §5.8 headline declaration

- regime axis (§5.1): **PARTIAL** (PARTIAL (sub-divided)); directedness axis (§5.2): **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** → combined: **Partial directed — a small undirected benefit ≪ directed, ≪ divided**.

> **HEADLINE (§5.8):** At the headline cell (sumprod, k=8, n=200), the early-position reveal-after B_k is **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** (95% CI [+1.2,+3.7]pp additive, agreeing on the logit scale), and the directedness contrast is **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** (CI [+14.3,+18.3]pp, with a significantly-positive reveal-before control [+17.2,+20.3]pp), giving the combined interpretation **Partial directed — a small undirected benefit ≪ directed, ≪ divided**. Qualifiers (§5.9): _not robust to held-out; position-sensitive_ — the full-sample additive early-pool is the point estimate; on Q1-only and the held-out subsample the regime reads NULL (both far below divided), so the precise NULL-vs-PARTIAL split is not robust, while the directedness conclusion and B_k ≪ divided are.** NB 'PARTIAL' here is statistically non-null (CI excludes 0 even at 98.3%) but scientifically NEAR-zero (~7% of B₁, ≪ divided) and fragile — NOT 'appreciable partial banking'; the held-out CI [-1.1,+2.9] still COVERS the full +2.5 (power-limited non-replication, not refutation).

_Contrast with vs without guard-#4 flagged rows: +16.2 [+14.3,+18.3] vs +16.5 [+14.5,+18.5] (immaterial)._

| 4 | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] | NEGATIVE | +0.1 [-2.9,+3.2] | NULL | early pool incl. transitional Q2 → position-sensitive |
| 8 | +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] | NULL | HEADLINE; clean Q1–Q4 |

## Supplementary within-scope diagnostics on the small headline +2.5 (mechanism of the near-null positive)

- **(1) Fixed-item corr(B_k, B₁)** (same revealed item alone at k=1 vs shared at k=8, n=100it; the exact item+control arm): Pearson r = **+0.005 [-0.058,+0.070]** over 1000 items → ≈ 0. **Largely UNINFORMATIVE, not directional evidence:** a per-item k=1 boost is a single Bernoulli difference (∈{−1,0,+1}), so any true latent correlation is heavily ATTENUATED toward 0 by measurement error regardless of mechanism — the tight CI rules out a large OBSERVED correlation, not a substantial latent one. Consistent with either reading; it only tells us the per-item structure of B₁ is not strongly visible in the noisy k=8 boost.
- **(2) k-gradient at matched per-question filler n/k=25 tok:** k=2 early B_k(=Q1) = +9.0 [+6.7,+11.2] vs k=8 early B_k(=Q1–Q4) = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7]. The boost is LARGER at the LESS-shared k=2, which is what sharing-limited genuine banking predicts. **At most WEAK/AMBIGUOUS evidence on the artifact, though:** an attention-redistribution artifact (caveat iv) could ALSO decrease with k — at higher k the reallocated attention is spread over a larger early pool (Q1 at k=2 vs Q1–Q4 at k=8), pushing the per-position artifact DOWN with k — so the artifact's k-dependence is itself ambiguous. (Composition note: k=2's early pool IS Q1, so this is ~a Q1-vs-Q1–Q4 read; the Q1-vs-Q1 read +9.0 vs +1.2 is even steeper. k=4 omitted — its early pool includes the transitional Q2, position-confounded.)
- **(3) Early-pool DEFINITION sensitivity (headline k=8, n=200):** Q1 +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6]; Q1–2 +2.6 [+0.9,+4.4]; Q1–3 +2.5 [+1.0,+3.9]; Q1–4 +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7]. The early-pool point estimate is ~stable (+2 to +2.5) across the boundary choice; the Q1-only → NULL split is a POWER difference (Q1-only has ¼ the rows, CI just covers 0), NOT a genuine within-band position dependence — the ⌈k/2⌉ boundary is not driving the result.

**Net on the +2.5 mechanism (analysis-only):** UNRESOLVED — the fixed-item corr≈0 is largely uninformative (attenuation), and the k-gradient is at most weak/ambiguous on the artifact; neither decisively bears either way. The attention-redistribution artifact (caveat iv) remains a LIVE possibility alongside weak genuine sharing-limited banking. The robust conclusions (B_k ≪ divided at ~10σ; DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE) do NOT depend on resolving it. A definitive attention/mechanism separation is a Segment-11 deep-dive.

content.text: My quick recompute (+1.6 early, +3.3 Q1) differs from the verdict (+2.5 early, +1.2 Q1). I need to understand the exact estimator. Let me read the analysis code.

"""Segment 8 — decision-rule machinery for the pre-registered §5 verdict (fast; reads JSONL, NO API
calls). Builds on `seg7_lib` (load/eff/early_labels/cluster constants) and adds:

  - richer bootstraps that return the FULL resample distribution (so any percentile + the SE are
    available — the §5.1 comparison-SE needs the cluster-bootstrap SE, and §5.6 needs the 98.3%/z=2.39
    Bonferroni CI, not just 95%);
  - the k=1 B₁(n) reference curve as a BOOTSTRAPPED, INTERPOLATED reference on BOTH the additive and the
    logit (Haldane) scale → genuine SE_R of the interpolated reference at the realized n / n/k (NOT 0);
  - the §5.1 ordered mutually-exclusive regime partition (additive + logit), with the TOST-style
    equivalence margin δ (additive 5pp; logit = the per-cell logit shift of a 5pp additive change at the
    cell's n=0 accuracy);
  - the §5.2 contrast adjudication, the §5.9 two-way (instance+item) variance (Cameron–Gelbach–Miller),
    and helpers for the §5.3 α positivity-gate / fraction-of-boost-retained.

The pre-registered inference unit is the drawn INSTANCE (cluster bootstrap, seed 0, percentile CIs).
"""
import math
from collections import defaultdict

import numpy as np

import seg7_lib as S

Z95 = 1.959963985
Z983 = 2.394489 # Bonferroni 3-test: 1 - 0.05/3 = 0.98333 two-sided -> z = 2.3944 (98.33% CI)
DELTA_ADD = 0.05      # additive equivalence margin δ = 5pp (in proportion units)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# bootstraps that expose the full resample distribution (mean, SE, arbitrary percentile CI)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
def cluster_boot_dist(clusters, seed=0, nb=4000):
    """Cluster bootstrap of the MEAN over instance clusters (list of np.arrays). Returns
    (point_mean, boot_array). point_mean is the observed mean; boot_array is the nb resample means."""
    arrs = [np.asarray(c, float) for c in clusters if len(c)]
    if not arrs:
        return float("nan"), np.array([])
    flat = np.concatenate(arrs)
    nclu = len(arrs)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    if nclu > 1:
        bs = np.array([np.concatenate([arrs[j] for j in rng.integers(0, nclu, nclu)]).mean()
                       for _ in range(nb)])
    else:
        bs = flat[rng.integers(0, len(flat), (nb, len(flat)))].mean(1)
    return float(flat.mean()), bs


def ci_se(point, boot, z=Z95):
    """Given a point estimate + bootstrap array, return (lo, hi) percentile CI matching two-sided z and
    the bootstrap SE. The percentile alpha is derived from z so 95%/98.3% share one code path."""
    if len(boot) == 0:
        return float("nan"), float("nan"), float("nan")
    # two-sided coverage for this z
    from math import erf, sqrt
    cov = erf(z / sqrt(2))                 # central coverage, e.g. z=1.96 -> 0.95
    lo_p, hi_p = 100 * (1 - cov) / 2, 100 * (1 + cov) / 2
    return float(np.percentile(boot, lo_p)), float(np.percentile(boot, hi_p)), float(boot.std(ddof=1))


def paired_diff_dist(clusters_a, clusters_b, seed=0, nb=4000):
    """Cluster bootstrap of (mean_b - mean_a) over SHARED instance keys (paired). Returns
    (point_diff, boot_array)."""
    insts = sorted(set(clusters_a) & set(clusters_b))
    if not insts:
        return float("nan"), np.array([])
    a_arr = [np.asarray(clusters_a[i], float) for i in insts]
    b_arr = [np.asarray(clusters_b[i], float) for i in insts]
    ma = np.concatenate(a_arr).mean()
    mb = np.concatenate(b_arr).mean()
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    nclu = len(insts)
    diffs = np.empty(nb)
    for t in range(nb):
        idx = rng.integers(0, nclu, nclu)
        va = np.concatenate([a_arr[j] for j in idx]).mean()
        vb = np.concatenate([b_arr[j] for j in idx]).mean()
        diffs[t] = vb - va
    return float(mb - ma), diffs


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# boost extraction that RETAINS the revealed-item id (for the §5.9 two-way instance+item bootstrap)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
def pool_labels(k, pool):
    if pool == "early":
        return S.early_labels(k)
    if pool == "q1":
        return {1}
    if pool == "agg":
        return None
    if isinstance(pool, int):
        return {pool}
    raise ValueError(pool)


def boost_records(rows, k, size, pool="early", inst_min=0):
    """List of dicts {inst, item, label, boost} for the requested position pool at filler `size` vs 0.
    `item` = the revealed item id at that position (item_ids[reveal_index]) — the unit for the item
    bootstrap. Scoring = the locked per-family scorer via seg7_lib.eff."""
    cells = defaultdict(dict)
    item_of = {}
    for r in rows:
        if r["k"] != k or r["inst"] < inst_min:
            continue
        key = (r["inst"], r["reveal_label"])
        cells[key][r["filler_items"]] = S.eff(r)
        item_of[key] = r["item_ids"][r["reveal_index"]]
    keep = pool_labels(k, pool)
    recs = []
    for (inst, label), sm in cells.items():
        if keep is not None and label not in keep:
            continue
        if 0 in sm and size in sm:
            recs.append(dict(inst=inst, item=item_of[(inst, label)], label=label, boost=sm[size] - sm[0]))
    return recs


def recs_to_inst_clusters(recs):
    by = defaultdict(list)
    for d in recs:
        by[d["inst"]].append(d["boost"])
    return {i: np.array(v, float) for i, v in by.items()}


def recs_to_item_clusters(recs):
    by = defaultdict(list)
    for d in recs:
        by[d["item"]].append(d["boost"])
    return {i: np.array(v, float) for i, v in by.items()}


def two_way_se(recs, seed=0, nb=4000):
    """Cameron–Gelbach–Miller two-way (instance + item) cluster-robust SE for the mean boost:
    SE_2way² = SE_inst² + SE_item² − SE_iid², floored at SE_inst² (the dominant clustering). Returns
    (mean, SE_2way, SE_inst, SE_item, SE_iid)."""
    vals = np.array([d["boost"] for d in recs], float)
    if len(vals) == 0:
        return (float("nan"),) * 5
    inst_cl = recs_to_inst_clusters(recs)
    item_cl = recs_to_item_clusters(recs)
    m_inst, b_inst = cluster_boot_dist([inst_cl[i] for i in sorted(inst_cl)], seed=seed, nb=nb)
    m_item, b_item = cluster_boot_dist([item_cl[i] for i in sorted(item_cl)], seed=seed + 1, nb=nb)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed + 2)
    b_iid = vals[rng.integers(0, len(vals), (nb, len(vals)))].mean(1)
    se_inst, se_item, se_iid = b_inst.std(ddof=1), b_item.std(ddof=1), b_iid.std(ddof=1)
    se2 = se_inst**2 + se_item**2 - se_iid**2
    se_2way = math.sqrt(max(se2, se_inst**2))
    return float(vals.mean()), float(se_2way), float(se_inst), float(se_item), float(se_iid)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# k=1 B₁(n) reference curve — bootstrapped + interpolated on additive AND logit scales
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _haldane_logit_acc(c, n):
    return math.log((c + 0.5) / (n - c + 0.5))


class K1Curve:
    """The k=1 reveal-after B₁(n) curve as a bootstrap-resampled, interpolated reference.

    Resamples the k=1 drawn instances (the pre-registered cluster unit) nb times; within each resample
    recomputes the per-grid-point B₁ (additive) and the Haldane-logit boost, then LINEARLY interpolates
    at an arbitrary target realized-token n. So `ref(n)` returns (R̂, SE_R) where SE_R is the genuine
    bootstrap SE of the *interpolated* reference (captures both sampling AND the interpolation position),
    not 0. The realized-token x-grid is the per-cell input_tokens delta (a fixed descriptive mapping)."""

    def __init__(self, level, seed=0, nb=4000):
        _, r1 = S.load(S.fpath(level, 1, "reveal_after"))
        sizes = [s for s in S.AFTER_SIZES[1] if s != 0]
        # per-instance eff at each size (incl 0); 1 position per instance for k=1
        eff0 = {}
        effs = {s: {} for s in sizes}
        toks = {0: [], **{s: [] for s in sizes}}
        for r in r1:
            if r["k"] != 1:
                continue
            i = r["inst"]
            s = r["filler_items"]
            if s == 0:
                eff0[i] = S.eff(r)
                toks[0].append(r["input_tokens"])
            elif s in effs:
                effs[s][i] = S.eff(r)
                toks[s].append(r["input_tokens"])
        insts = sorted(set(eff0) & set.intersection(*[set(effs[s]) for s in sizes]))
        self.rt = np.array([np.mean(toks[s]) - np.mean(toks[0]) for s in sizes])  # realized tok per grid pt
        order = np.argsort(self.rt)
        self.rt = self.rt[order]
        sizes_o = [sizes[j] for j in order]
        # matrices [n_inst x n_sizes]
        E0 = np.array([eff0[i] for i in insts])
        EN = np.array([[effs[s][i] for s in sizes_o] for i in insts])  # [inst, size]
        boosts = EN - E0[:, None]                                       # additive per-instance boost
        # point estimates
        self.add_pt = boosts.mean(0)
        n_inst = len(insts)
        acc0_pt = E0.mean()
        accN_pt = EN.mean(0)
        self.logit_pt = np.array([_haldane_logit_acc(accN_pt[j] * n_inst, n_inst) -
                                  _haldane_logit_acc(acc0_pt * n_inst, n_inst) for j in range(len(sizes_o))])
        # bootstrap resamples
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.add_bs = np.empty((nb, len(sizes_o)))
        self.logit_bs = np.empty((nb, len(sizes_o)))
        for t in range(nb):
            idx = rng.integers(0, n_inst, n_inst)
            self.add_bs[t] = boosts[idx].mean(0)
            a0 = E0[idx].mean()
            aN = EN[idx].mean(0)
            self.logit_bs[t] = [_haldane_logit_acc(aN[j] * n_inst, n_inst) -
                                _haldane_logit_acc(a0 * n_inst, n_inst) for j in range(len(sizes_o))]
        self.acc0_pt = acc0_pt
        self.accN_pt = accN_pt

    def ref_add(self, n):
        """(R̂, SE_R) additive at realized-token target n (linear interp; SE_R = bootstrap SD of the
        interpolated value)."""
        rhat = float(np.interp(n, self.rt, self.add_pt))
        bs = np.array([np.interp(n, self.rt, self.add_bs[t]) for t in range(self.add_bs.shape[0])])
        return rhat, float(bs.std(ddof=1))

    def ref_logit(self, n):
        rhat = float(np.interp(n, self.rt, self.logit_pt))
        bs = np.array([np.interp(n, self.rt, self.logit_bs[t]) for t in range(self.logit_bs.shape[0])])
        return rhat, float(bs.std(ddof=1))

    def acc_at(self, n):
        """Interpolated k=1 accuracy at realized n (for logit-reference reporting / δ context)."""
        return float(np.interp(n, np.concatenate([[0.0], self.rt]),
                               np.concatenate([[self.acc0_pt], self.accN_pt])))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# the §5.1 ordered mutually-exclusive regime partition (one scale)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
def classify_regime(bk, bk_lo, bk_hi, se_bk, refs, z, delta):
    """refs = {'null':(R̂,SE_R), 'divided':(R̂,SE_R), 'parallel':(R̂,SE_R)}; all in the SAME scale/units
    as bk. Returns (category, detail dict). Ordered partition, first match wins (§5.1)."""
    par_hat = refs["parallel"][0]

    def cmp(Rhat, SE_R):
        d = bk - Rhat
        hw = z * math.sqrt(se_bk**2 + SE_R**2)
        dlo, dhi = d - hw, d + hw
        excludes = (dlo > 0) or (dhi < 0)
        consistent = (dlo <= 0 <= dhi) and (dlo > -delta) and (dhi < delta)
        return excludes, consistent, (dlo, dhi)

    exN, conN, dN = cmp(*refs["null"])
    exD, conD, dD = cmp(*refs["divided"])
    exP, conP, dP = cmp(*refs["parallel"])
    detail = dict(excl_null=exN, cons_null=conN, excl_div=exD, cons_div=conD,
                  excl_par=exP, cons_par=conP, cmp_null=dN, cmp_div=dD, cmp_par=dP)
    if bk_lo > par_hat:
        return "SUPER-PARALLEL/ARTIFACT", detail
    if bk_hi < 0:
        return "NEGATIVE", detail
    if conN and exD:
        return "NULL", detail
    if conP and exD:
        return "PARALLEL", detail
    if bk_lo > 0 and conD and exP:
        return "DIVIDED", detail
    if bk_lo > 0 and exN and exD and exP:
        side = "sub-divided" if bk < refs["divided"][0] else "super-divided/sub-parallel"
        return f"PARTIAL ({side})", detail
    return "AMBIGUOUS", detail


def classify_contrast(c_lo, c_hi, delta=DELTA_ADD):
    """§5.2 contrast classification (the CI is the contrast = before − after)."""
    if c_lo > 0:
        return "DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE"
    if c_hi < 0:
        return "DIRECTEDNESS-NEGATIVE"
    if (c_lo <= 0 <= c_hi) and (c_lo > -delta) and (c_hi < delta):
        return "NO-DIRECTEDNESS-EFFECT"
    return "AMBIGUOUS"


def delta_logit(p0):
    """Per-cell logit equivalence margin = logit(p0+0.05) − logit(p0) (§5.4)."""
    p0 = min(max(p0, 1e-6), 1 - 0.05 - 1e-6)
    return math.log((p0 + 0.05) / (1 - p0 - 0.05)) - math.log(p0 / (1 - p0))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cell-level logit boost on the early pool (Haldane), cluster-bootstrapped over instances
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
def early_pool_acc_rows(rows, k, size, pool="early", inst_min=0):
    """Return {inst: (list of eff at size, list of eff at 0)} for the pool — used to bootstrap the
    cell's Haldane-logit boost (resample instances, recompute pooled acc_n / acc_0)."""
    keep = pool_labels(k, pool)
    byk = defaultdict(lambda: {"n": [], "0": []})
    cells = defaultdict(dict)
    for r in rows:
        if r["k"] != k or r["inst"] < inst_min:
            continue
        if keep is not None and r["reveal_label"] not in keep:
            continue
        cells[(r["inst"], r["reveal_label"])][r["filler_items"]] = S.eff(r)
    for (inst, label), sm in cells.items():
        if 0 in sm and size in sm:
            byk[inst]["n"].append(sm[size])
            byk[inst]["0"].append(sm[0])
    return {i: (np.array(v["n"], float), np.array(v["0"], float)) for i, v in byk.items()}


def logit_contrast_dist(ra, rb, k, size, pool="early", seed=0, nb=4000):
    """PAIRED cluster bootstrap of the Haldane-LOGIT directedness contrast (before − after) over the
    SHARED instance set. Each resample recomputes the pooled early-pool acc_n/acc_0 for each arm from the
    SAME resampled instances (so the contrast SE captures the before/after covariance), then the logit
    boost of each arm. Returns (contrast_point, boot_array, before_logit_pt, after_logit_pt). Used for the
    §5.6 d6 cross-family confirmatory test on the logit scale (d6's k=8 reveal-before Q4 ceilings at 95%,
    so the additive contrast is headroom-confounded; the logit/Haldane contrast is the honest read)."""
    a = early_pool_acc_rows(ra, k, size, pool=pool)
    b = early_pool_acc_rows(rb, k, size, pool=pool)
    insts = sorted(set(a) & set(b))
    if not insts:
        return float("nan"), np.array([]), float("nan"), float("nan")

    def lboost(arrs_n, arrs_0):
        cn = np.concatenate(arrs_n)
        c0 = np.concatenate(arrs_0)
        N = len(cn)
        return _haldane_logit_acc(cn.mean() * N, N) - _haldane_logit_acc(c0.mean() * N, N)

    an = [a[i][0] for i in insts]; a0 = [a[i][1] for i in insts]
    bn = [b[i][0] for i in insts]; b0 = [b[i][1] for i in insts]
    after_pt, before_pt = lboost(an, a0), lboost(bn, b0)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    nclu = len(insts)
    bs = np.empty(nb)
    for t in range(nb):
        idx = rng.integers(0, nclu, nclu)
        af = lboost([an[j] for j in idx], [a0[j] for j in idx])
        bf = lboost([bn[j] for j in idx], [b0[j] for j in idx])
        bs[t] = bf - af
    return float(before_pt - after_pt), bs, float(before_pt), float(after_pt)


def logit_boost_dist(rows, k, size, pool="early", inst_min=0, seed=0, nb=4000):
    """Cluster-bootstrap distribution of the early-pool Haldane-logit boost. Returns (point, boot_array,
    p0_point) where p0_point is the pooled n=0 accuracy (for δ_logit)."""
    by = early_pool_acc_rows(rows, k, size, pool=pool, inst_min=inst_min)
    insts = sorted(by)
    if not insts:
        return float("nan"), np.array([]), float("nan")
    nlist = [by[i][0] for i in insts]
    zlist = [by[i][1] for i in insts]
    all_n = np.concatenate(nlist)
    all_0 = np.concatenate(zlist)
    N = len(all_n)
    p0 = all_0.mean()
    pn = all_n.mean()
    point = _haldane_logit_acc(pn * N, N) - _haldane_logit_acc(p0 * N, N)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    nclu = len(insts)
    bs = np.empty(nb)
    for t in range(nb):
        idx = rng.integers(0, nclu, nclu)
        cn = np.concatenate([nlist[j] for j in idx])
        c0 = np.concatenate([zlist[j] for j in idx])
        M = len(cn)
        bs[t] = _haldane_logit_acc(cn.mean() * M, M) - _haldane_logit_acc(c0.mean() * M, M)
    return float(point), bs, float(p0)

# Segment 10 — robustness verdict (Opus 4.5, sumprod_t3_d2) — EXPLORATORY ROBUSTNESS

> **⚑ These are EXPLORATORY / CONFIRMATORY-ROBUSTNESS analyses, NOT the pre-registered confirmatory family.** The locked `results/preregistration.md` confirmatory family is fixed. Here we test whether the *already-declared* evolved headline — **at high k the UNDIRECTED per-question filler boost is near-null / `B_k << divided` while the DIRECTEDNESS contrast (reveal-before − reveal-after) is POSITIVE**, read position-resolved (early-pool + Q1-only) on additive AND logit — SURVIVES the three pre-planned variations. No α-fit (ill-defined at the null); no re-declaration; no goalpost-moving.

_Machinery mirrors Segment 8 (`seg10_lib` -> `seg8_lib`/`seg9_lib`): cluster bootstrap over drawn instances (4000 resamples, seed 0; 95% percentile CIs); the comparison CI is (B_k−R̂) ± z·√(SE_Bk²+SE_R²) with SE_R the genuine bootstrap SE of the interpolated k=1 reference; robust scorer for sumprod; early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ listed positions, Q1-only co-reported; additive AND logit (Haldane). Told/counting baseline = the cached Segment-7 cells (reused, not re-run)._


## V1 — told-vs-NOT-TOLD framing ablation (HIGHEST priority; counting filler)

_The 'told' framing tells the model up front it will be asked exactly ONE of these (be ready for any); 'not_told' removes that encouragement. **reveal_before is FRAMING-INVARIANT** (it names the target in turn 1 regardless of framing) -> the not_told reveal_before cells are byte-identical to the told (Segment-7) cells and cache-hit; so the told->not_told manipulation bites ONLY on the reveal_after arm, and the directedness contrast under not_told differs from told ONLY via reveal_after. The regime references use the matching k=1 curve (not_told k=1 for the not_told read)._

### reveal_after n=0 early-pool accuracy: does removing the encouragement shift the baseline?

| k | told n=0 (early) | not-told n=0 (early) | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 39.1% | 36.8% | -2.3pp |
| 4 | 30.4% | 32.4% | +2.0pp |
| 8 | 22.3% | 27.2% | +4.9pp |

### Undirected reveal-after B_k + directedness contrast (told vs not-told)

_The **early pool** (first ⌈k/2⌉ positions) is the Segment-8 PRIMARY position-resolved read; Q1-only is the co-report. **Key:** the directedness contrast on the PRIMARY early-pool read SURVIVES not-told (weakened, sig.+ at k=4,8); it collapses only on the Q1-only co-report (because Q1 is the model's default undirected target — see the disclosure disambiguation below). What BREAKS is the undirected near-null REGIME leg (B_k reaches ≈divided)._

| k | scale | TOLD B_k (early) | NOT-TOLD B_k (early) | NOT-TOLD div ref | NOT-TOLD B_k<<div? | TOLD contrast | NOT-TOLD contrast | NOT-TOLD before ctrl (g#1) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | add | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] PARTIAL | +22.9 [+20.5,+25.4] PARTIAL | +26.9 | YES | +10.9 [+7.8,+13.9] POSITIVE | -4.2 [-7.3,-1.3] NEGATIVE | +18.8 [+16.3,+21.1] PASS |
| 2 | logit | +0.32 [+0.23,+0.41] | +0.94 [+0.83,+1.04] | +1.12 | — | +0.4 [+0.3,+0.6] | -0.2 [-0.3,-0.1] | +0.76 |
| 2 | Q1-add | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] PARTIAL | +22.9 [+20.5,+25.4] PARTIAL | +26.9 | YES | — | — | — |
| 4 | add | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] NEGATIVE | +11.2 [+8.8,+13.5] PARTIAL | +26.9 | YES | +24.2 [+21.2,+27.2] POSITIVE | +9.1 [+5.8,+12.0] POSITIVE | +20.2 [+17.8,+22.5] PASS |
| 4 | logit | -0.20 [-0.30,-0.09] | +0.48 [+0.38,+0.58] | +1.12 | — | +1.0 [+0.9,+1.2] | +0.4 [+0.2,+0.5] | +0.83 |

| 8 | Q1-add | +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] NULL | +26.3 [+23.0,+29.6] PARTIAL | +10.2 | no | — | — | — |

### Directedness contrast: PRIMARY early-pool (survives) vs Q1-only co-report (collapses)

_The Q1-only collapse is NOT 'directedness is fake': it's that Q1 is the default undirected target (directing to Q1 adds nothing), while directing to the NON-default Q2–Q4 still helps — which is exactly why the early-pool contrast stays +9–10pp. **The not-told contrast is SMALL only because the not-told reveal_after baseline ALREADY defaults to Q1** (it lacks the disclosure). The genuinely CONFOUND-MINIMAL directedness test is **reveal_before − disclose** (both arms carry the 'one-of-k chosen at random' disclosure, so the disclose arm does NOT default to Q1 — they differ ONLY by whether the target is named): it is LARGE and framing-robust — **k=4 early +24.8 / Q1 +27.7; k=8 early +16.2 / Q1 +23.7** (sig.+). So 'directing latent compute to a named target' is robust and large (~+24) against any baseline that does not already default to that target; it reads +9–10 against the not-told baseline only because not-told pre-banks Q1._

| k | TOLD contrast early | NOT-TOLD contrast early [class] | TOLD contrast Q1 | NOT-TOLD contrast Q1 [class] |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | +10.9 [+7.8,+13.9] | -4.2 [-7.3,-1.3] **NEGATIVE** | +10.9 [+7.8,+13.9] | -4.2 [-7.3,-1.3] **NEGATIVE** |
| 4 | +24.2 [+21.2,+27.2] | +9.1 [+5.8,+12.0] **POSITIVE** | +27.1 [+22.7,+31.4] | -3.1 [-7.7,+1.4] **AMBIGUOUS** |
| 8 | +16.2 [+14.3,+18.3] | +10.2 [+8.2,+12.3] **POSITIVE** | +24.5 [+20.5,+28.5] | -0.6 [-5.1,+4.1] **AMBIGUOUS** |

### Disambiguation — is the Q1-suppression the random-target DISCLOSURE or the ENCOURAGEMENT?

_The `disclose` arm = the told prompt MINUS the '— so be ready to answer any of them' encouragement clause (so it DISCLOSES 'one of these, chosen at random, will be asked' but does NOT encourage readiness). told−disclose = the ENCOURAGEMENT effect; disclose−not_told = the DISCLOSURE effect. (Genuine no-CoT re-verified: reason 0%, viol <0.12%.)_

| k | told Q1 B_k | disclose Q1 B_k | not-told Q1 B_k | driver |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | +0.1 [-2.9,+3.2] | -0.5 [-3.4,+2.5] | +30.3 [+26.9,+33.8] | disclose≈told≪not-told → the **DISCLOSURE** suppresses Q1, NOT the encouragement |
| 8 | +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] | +2.0 [-0.6,+4.5] | +26.3 [+23.0,+29.6] | disclose≈told≪not-told → the **DISCLOSURE** suppresses Q1, NOT the encouragement |

### Per-position reveal-after boost at k=8 (200 tok): WHERE does the not-told boost go?

| pos | told n=0 | not-told n=0 | told B_k | not-told B_k |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | 20.3% | 20.5% | +1.2 | +26.3 |
| Q2 | 21.4% | 28.1% | +4.0 | +1.0 |
| Q3 | 24.6% | 28.0% | +2.2 | +5.6 |
| Q4 | 22.9% | 32.3% | +2.5 | +0.9 |
| Q5 | 26.3% | 24.1% | -4.4 | +4.5 |
| Q6 | 39.4% | 46.9% | -15.9 | -17.8 |
| Q7 | 51.4% | 54.8% | -22.8 | -25.4 |
| Q8 | 45.2% | 46.6% | -17.3 | -19.0 |

_Dose-response (not-told Q1, k=8): boost +18.7 @100 tok → +26.3 @200 tok (scales with n → a genuine FILLER effect, not just 'answers Q1 better')._

_Suppression, not redistribution: the **clean Q1 term** (identical n=0 baselines 20.3% vs 20.5%) is +1.2 (told) vs +26.3 (not-told) — told banks far LESS on Q1, it does not redistribute it. (The summed Q1–Q4 additive +9.9 vs +33.8 points the same way but is partly headroom-mixed — not-told Q2/Q4 n=0 are ~7–9pp higher — so we lead with the baseline-matched Q1 term and the logit scale, which agree.)_


**V1 read — the told prompt is LOAD-BEARING via the random-target DISCLOSURE (the encouragement is INCIDENTAL).** Under not-told the model concentrates its filler benefit on its DEFAULT first-listed question Q1 (k=8 Q1 boost +26.3, n-scaling, ≈ the directed reveal-before Q1 boost +25.7). The **disclosure disambiguation** pins the driver: the `disclose` arm (told MINUS the encouragement) behaves like TOLD (Q1 +2.0 ≈ told +1.2 ≪ not-told +26.3), so it is the DISCLOSURE that 'one of these, chosen at random, will be asked' — NOT the 'be ready for any' encouragement — that SUPPRESSES the model's Q1-default banking (plausibly: once any question may be quizzed, Q1 is no longer a safe default to invest the filler in). This RESOLVES the earlier 'three things change at once' caveat. (The not-told Q1 boost +26.3 is ~68% of the framing-matched not-told lone +38.5 — ~one question's worth.)

**What BREAKS vs SURVIVES (be precise — a review correction):**
- The undirected **near-null REGIME leg BREAKS**: not-told Q1 +26.3 EXCEEDS divided (super-divided), and the early-pool average +8.5 reaches ≈divided — so the original *interpretation* 'the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation' is **REFUTED** (it banks ~one question's worth, on Q1). NB the position-balanced early-pool average landing AT divided is exactly why **position-resolution is load-bearing**: only resolving by position reveals this is Q1-CONCENTRATION, not an even divided-pool split.
- The **directedness-contrast leg SURVIVES (weakened) on the PRIMARY early-pool read** (not-told k=4,8 contrast +9.1/+10.2, sig.+, vs told +24.2/+16.2); it collapses only on the Q1-only co-report (AMBIGUOUS at k=4,8 — CI covers 0 but its lower bound exceeds the −5pp equivalence margin, so NOT a confirmed null; k=4 −7.7 is solid, k=8 −5.1 is knife-edge; a small significant REVERSAL at k=2 −4.2). The Q1 collapse is because Q1 is the model's DEFAULT undirected target under not-told — directing to Q1 adds nothing, while directing to Q2–Q4 still helps (hence the early pool stays +). **The genuinely confound-minimal directedness test is reveal_before − disclose** (both arms carry the random-target disclosure, so the disclose baseline does NOT default to Q1 — they differ ONLY by naming the target): it is LARGE and framing-robust (k=4 Q1 +27.7, k=8 Q1 +23.7; early +24.8/+16.2). So 'directing latent compute to a named target' is robust and large (~+24) against any baseline that doesn't already default to that target; the +9–10 not-told number is small ONLY because not-told pre-banks Q1. The earlier claim that the contrast 'vanishes / is a told-framing artifact' was WRONG and is retracted.
- The deeper **'NO PARALLEL latent thinking across k' SURVIVES / is REINFORCED**: under NEITHER framing does the model bank >~1 question's worth (not-told concentrates ~1 on the single Q1; told suppresses even that). The headline PATTERN (near-null + directedness-positive package) is framing-dependent — carried by the regime/near-null leg breaking — but the anti-parallel conclusion and the directed-compute-to-a-named-target finding both hold.


## V2 — alternative k in {3,6,16} (told, counting): is the trend a k in {2,4,8} artifact?

_The cleanest cross-k view holds TOTAL filler FIXED at 200 tok (size 100 counting items) and reads the undirected per-question boost as more questions share the SAME pool. k in {2,4,8} are read from the cached Segment-7 cells; k in {3,6,16} are new. A smooth monotone decline across k in {2,3,4,6,8} confirms the divided-pool-shaped sharing decline isn't a k-artifact. Reference = the Segment-7 told/counting k=1 curve. (k=16: n/16=12.5 tok < n_lo -> its divided reference sits in the small-n dip and is ill-defined; reported for the decline-extension only.)_

Kimi K2 is throttled to 50 RPM on OpenRouter — too slow for dense sweeps (an availability strike). Let me note that and pivot to DeepSeek V3-0324 (clean, non-reasoning, fast provider). Let me check the partial state and run DeepSeek calibration.

Let me make the wrapper retry transient provider errors, build a robust open-model analysis lib, then (a) amplify DeepSeek-V3-0324 (the cleanest candidate) with larger filler + dots and (b) survey more models.
**Selection decision: DeepSeek-V3-0324.** It has the biggest clean boost (dots +12.5pp@add_n4_d10 vs kimi's +5.9pp), is ~2× faster, cheaper, more reliable (no rate limits/provider errors), and cleaner outputs. Now let me run the final confirmatory DeepSeek runs (n=1000, full rising-region + tail, counting & dots) plus a canonical floor check.
The truncation (~3%) is benign: the model emits the **answer first**, then rambles (continuing the dots pattern or commentary) — first-integer parse correctly captures the answer-first token. A rare ~0.1% emit a wrong small number first (conservative false-negatives, biasing boost *down*). Let me quantify "answer-is-first-token" rigorously and confirm the boost is robust to truncation.
**V3.2 disqualification confirmed**: 2.82% letters-before-first-digit (vs DeepSeek-V3-0324's 0.083%) — explicit CoT ("We are asked to compute...", "Let's add step by step", "Step 0... Step 4...") *before* the answer, plus one 7073-token essay (provider ignored max_tokens & prefill). Provider-dependent and unreliable. Let me verify the Qwen3-235B negative is genuine (clean no-CoT, not a bug).
**Kimi-k2-0905 has ~10% empty responses** (in_tok=0, out_tok=0, raw='') — provider failures in its OpenRouter pool that my wrapper wrongly accepted. DeepSeek-V3-0324 has none (0.03% parse-fail). This is a real wrapper robustness gap: empty/degenerate completions should be retried (re-routed), not cached. Let me add that guard (won't affect DeepSeek's valid cache).
Three excellent, consistent reviews — no blocking issues, but a clear set of should-fix items (raised across reviewers). The biggest: **provider routing is unpinned** (multi-provider/quantization noise; pairing not truly paired). Let me start by analyzing the provider distribution (no API) to verify size-invariance and pick a provider to pin.
**Critical confound confirmed (reviewers were right):** providers split into a high-accuracy cluster (ModelRun/DeepInfra/SiliconFlow ≈54–55%) and a heavily-quantized low-accuracy cluster (AtlasCloud/Novita/GMICloud ≈22–29%), AND the mix is **size-dependent** — the bad providers serve ~45% of *baseline* (size-0) calls but only ~20% at filler sizes 3–150. This **inflates the boost**. I must re-run pinned to one provider. Let me add provider-pinning to the runners and verify a good provider.
Let me verify DeepInfra (a high-accuracy provider) honors prefill + reasoning-off when pinned, with a tiny test, then launch the full pinned re-run.
Provider pinning works cleanly (DeepInfra/ModelRun/SiliconFlow all honor prefill, reasoning off; same item differs across providers → confirms quantization differences). Let me add big-answer levels + provider/big-grid options to the calibrator, then run a pinned DeepInfra calibration to pick clean mid-band levels.
Pinned to DeepInfra, **add_n4_d10 baseline is 57%** (vs 39% unpinned) — confirming the unpinned boost was inflated by the bad-provider-heavy baseline. Mid-band big-answer levels (pinned): add_n4_d10 (57%), add_n4_d12 (49%), add_n5_d10 (34%). Let me run the clean pinned boost across these + add_n5_d8 (dots & counting, n=1000).

Pinned runs are slower (~26/s on single-provider DeepInfra, ~28 min) — let me let them run and do analysis work. First, save the gpt-oss-120b / llama-3.3-70b disqualification evidence to a results file.
gpt-oss-120b ("Reasoning is mandatory" 400) and llama-3.3-70b (shows working → first integer = operand 12345678) evidence saved. Now let me write a robustness analysis (logit-scale boost + error-structure + provider-stratified table) to run on the pinned data when ready.
The single-DeepInfra pin is rate-limited (ETA 4h). Better approach: restrict to the **high-accuracy provider cluster** {DeepInfra, ModelRun, SiliconFlow} (all ~54–55%, so no quantization confound) for ~3× throughput. Let me kill the slow runs and test provider allowlisting.
`only` still routes everything to DeepInfra (no throughput gain). Better: **denylist the low-accuracy providers** (`ignore: [atlascloud, novita, gmicloud]`, allow_fallbacks=true) — this keeps the homogeneous good cluster (DeepInfra/ModelRun/SiliconFlow, all ~54–55%, no confound) and load-balances for throughput. Let me add denylist support and test it spreads.
The `ignore` denylist slugs didn't match (AtlasCloud still served 41/120). Let me use the **`only` allowlist** with the good cluster instead (guaranteed to never hit bad providers) and test whether it spreads for throughput at high concurrency.
`only:[deepinfra,modelrun,siliconflow]` spreads across the 3 good providers (~54–55%, homogeneous) and never hits the bad ones — ~10/s. Let me add `--only-providers` and run the clean headline experiment with the good cluster.
**Major correction (the provider confound was large):** with the clean good-cluster (truncation collapsed 3%→0.01%, confirming bad providers caused the pathological outputs), the boost is **smaller and narrower**: add_n4_d10 (base 52%) **counting +7.2pp [3.8,10.6], dots +5.0pp [1.7,8.2]** (both CIs exclude 0), but d12/d5_d10/d5_d8 are null. The unpinned +11pp was ~half provider-mix artifact, and even "dots>counting" reverses. Let me see the full clean curves and run the robustness analysis.

Error structure is strong evidence of genuine computation: **81% carry near-misses, 0% give-ups, filler fixes near-misses 2× more than far-misses**. The provider confound was real and large. # Segment 11 — MECHANISM & confound investigation: consolidated verdict

_EXPLORATORY mechanism work (locked plan/harness/scorers/pre-registration UNTOUCHED; no α-fit; no re-declaration). Reads: seg11_framing.md, seg11_mechanism.md, seg11_default.md, seg11_cap.md, seg11_dots.md, seg11_nocot.md. Genuine no-CoT re-verified for every new cell (seg11_nocot.md: 352k outputs, reason% 0, genuine-viol 0.019%, scoring 0-inconsistent)._


## TIER 1.1 — the not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression finding REPLICATES (in DIRECTION) cross-FAMILY (Opus d6) AND cross-MODEL (DeepSeek)

Undirected Q1 boost B_k at k=8 (operating point), told vs disclose vs not-told:

| level / model | told | disclose | not-told | divided ref | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sumprod (Opus, original) | +1.2 | +2.0 | **+26.3** [+23.0,+29.6] | +13.7 | not-told ≫ disclose ≈ told ✓ |
| add_n15_d6 (Opus, cross-family) | -2.2 | -3.4 | **+10.6** [+8.1,+13.1] | +15.0 | not-told ≫ disclose ≈ told ✓ |
| add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-model) | +3.4 | +2.2 | **+6.2** [+4.7,+7.8] | +7.7 | not-told > disclose ≈ told ✓ |

**REPLICATES (in DIRECTION).** On the NEW family (d6) and the NEW model (DeepSeek) — as on the original sumprod — the model commits a positive Q1 boost ONLY under not-told, and the random-target DISCLOSURE (disclose ≈ told) suppresses it. The reframe is **NOT sumprod-specific**. **But the magnitude ATTENUATES cross-family and especially cross-model even on the headroom-robust LOGIT scale** (not-told−told Δlogit: sumprod +1.16 ≫ d6 +0.52 ≫ DeepSeek +0.12) — the smaller cross-family/model effect is only PARTLY headroom (the additive numbers sumprod +26 / d6 +11 / DeepSeek +6 partly reflect Q1 headroom, but DeepSeek is ~10× weaker even on logit). **Honest scope (data-inspector):** the cleanest separation everywhere is **not-told ≫ disclose**; on DeepSeek not-told vs told CIs just touch (a weaker, partial cross-model leg, consistent with Segment 9). The **d6 leg rests on Q1 + logit alone** (its early-pool/aggregate is uninterpretable: later positions go to −60pp via carry-disruption×recency-erasure, carry-fragile regime); the DIRECTION is unambiguous (told −2.2/logit −0.09 -> not-told +10.6/logit +0.43). **Framing also shifts n0 slightly even with IDENTICAL draws** (d6 told 55% vs not-told 48%; DeepSeek 53 vs 58, opposite sign), but logit agrees and sumprod's baselines are EQUAL across framing yet show the LARGEST effect — so the Q1-banking is NOT a headroom artifact.)

## TIER 1.2 — genuine-hidden-computation vs attention/retrieval reallocation: STRENGTHENED (not proven, honestly bounded)

- **(A) DIRECTED dose-response (necessary, not sufficient):** the directed (reveal_before) Q1 boost RISES with filler tokens (−1.6→+27.1 over 24→300 tok) and TRACKS the lone B1(n) shape; the not-told committed Q1 boost likewise scales. A *pure-step* attention/recency-reallocation account predicts no graded n-dependence -> the graded rise DISFAVOURS a pure-step account, but a *graded* attention/length account can also scale with n, so this is necessary not sufficient (selectivity (B) is the stronger lever).
- **(B) compute-selectivity:** at a fixed directed cell the boost RISES with item difficulty (sumprod max-product quartile +6.7→+10.8→+14.7→+16.2; logit +0.27→+0.66) at a ~FLAT n0 baseline (52→48%) -> NOT headroom; filler selectively helps the more compute-bound items. **Cross-check ($0, cached directed cells, WITH the flat-baseline control):** the directed boost RISES with #carries on d6 (+11.0→+19.6) BUT d6's n0 also DECLINES ~8pp (74→66%) — a partial headroom confound (unlike sumprod's flat baseline); on DeepSeek the baseline IS flat but the boost is ALSO flat (~+7, no selectivity). So the CLEANEST difficulty-selectivity is sumprod (rising boost AT a flat baseline); the genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning, not uniformly cross-family/model clean.
- **(B2) directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated:** the (before−after) contrast is sig.+ at ALL 8 positions (+7 to +27pp), so directing to a NON-Q1 target gains everywhere (not merely 'position 1 is read best'); BUT the directed *absolute* boost is large early (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7, Q3 +20.6) and small at Q6/Q7 (+1.7/+2.4) — so directed efficacy is strongly primacy-graded (the large late-position contrast is carried by the negative reveal-after arm). The not-told Q1 default is positional because Q1 is the best GUESS when the model must commit before knowing the target.
- **(C) banks-vs-retrieves:** the not-told per-position profile is Q1 +26.3, Q2–Q5 ~+3, Q6–Q8 negative -> the model banks a SINGLE guessed target (its default Q1) and retrieves it IF asked; the small reveal-after AVERAGE is because the reveal is random. This REFUTES 'banks a diffuse all-questions computation it cannot retrieve'.
- **SUPPORTED:** genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** computation committed to ONE question — the NAMED target (reveal_before, target-following) or the **positional default Q1** (undirected). The model does NOT spread compute across the k questions (no parallel). **UNPROVEN / honest bounds (sharpened after LLM review):** (i) a PURE-STEP attention account is disfavoured (no graded n-dependence) but a GRADED attention/bandwidth ('longer-context read' or decode-time KV-buffer) account is NOT formally excluded — it can also yield a monotone dose + a difficulty gradient at flat baseline; selectivity is the stronger lever, not decisive. (ii) LOCALIZATION is not established: the data show filler ENABLES the benefit and that it scales with filler AMOUNT, but not that the computation happens AT the filler positions vs at decode reading them as scratch — hence 'filler-amount-dependent', NOT 'filler-time'. (iii) CONTENT vs length: dots (a different filler) also banks Q1 under not-told, so it is not counting-specific, but both are 'length' (a random-filler control + activation probing are the decisive future tests). (iv) **EVIDENCE BASE:** the dose-response + the CLEAN (flat-baseline) selectivity are Opus-sumprod-only (on d6 the selectivity rises but with a baseline confound; on DeepSeek it is absent) — the genuine-computation interpretation rests mainly on Opus. We do NOT claim parallel latent thinking and do NOT assert a proven mechanism.

## TIER 2.3 — the DEFAULT-TARGET (why Q1?) is POSITIONAL (primacy / first-listed)

Fixed-item re-ordering (SAME item at positions {Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8}, not-told k=8): B_k = +25.1 / −2.1 / +3.0 / −18.0 -> the boost follows the listed POSITION, not the content. Content cross-check (iid): Q1 boosted for BOTH easy (+27.7) and hard (+24.9) items, later positions banked for neither -> **positional/primacy default, NOT content-based**.

## TIER 2.4 — the CONTEXT-INTERFERENCE CAP is mostly STRUCTURE (the first distractor)

Directed (reveal_before) boost at a FIXED item/position vs #distractors (200 tok): lone (k=1) +43.0 -> k=2 +23.6 -> k=4 +30.1 -> k=8 +25.3 (logit +1.84 -> +0.97/+1.25/+1.03; LEAD with logit). The bulk of the cap appears IMMEDIATELY at the first distractor and then ~saturates -> the cap is **mostly the multi-question STRUCTURE, not progressive load**. Two honest refinements (reviewers): (i) part of the cap is **headroom pre-consumption** — the distractor questions act as preceding-context filler, raising the pos0 n0 (29%->38%) even at n=0; (ii) in REACHABLE-ACCURACY terms the gap is modest (in-prompt directed Q1 reaches ~63% = 38+25 vs lone ~72% = 29+43, ~9pp), so 'roughly halves the boost' is a boost/logit-space statement — the cleaner mechanism is 'part of the budget is already spent on questions-as-filler'.

## TIER 3 — light probes

- **dots×k (3.5):** the dots disruption is FRAMING-INDEPENDENT, a ~6-7pp uniform penalty vs counting that roughly doubles from k=1 (~+3.5pp gap) to k=8. Under not-told dots ALSO concentrates a large committed Q1 boost (+18.2 vs counting +26.3) -> dots is NOT a categorical 'no-op/disengagement' signal, just a weaker k-structure compute substrate. Bounded; does not change any verdict (only deepens B_k ≪ divided under told).
- **d6 carry anomaly (3.6):** under not-told d6's later positions go strongly negative (k=8 Q8 −60pp) — a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture far larger than sumprod's; the structured d6 per-position swing remains an OPEN PUZZLE (tied to the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit saturation anomaly), documented for the synthesis. It does not bear on the Q1-banking headline (we lead d6 with Q1 + logit).

## Net

The evolved framing headline (not-told -> Q1-default banking; disclosure suppresses it) **generalizes IN DIRECTION** cross-family + cross-model (magnitude attenuates even on logit; DeepSeek partial); the filler benefit is **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent computation** committed to a SINGLE question (the named target — target-following but primacy-modulated; or the positional default Q1), **NOT parallel**, **not a pure-step attention artifact** (though a graded attention account is not formally excluded and localization is not established); the default target is **positional**; the in-prompt directed boost is **capped at ~half the lone** by the multi-question structure (one distractor). All EXPLORATORY; genuine no-CoT holds throughout.
